name: AC-1
practice_description: The organization defines the personnel or roles to be recipients of the access control policy necessary to facilitate the implementation of the access control policy and associated access controls.

name: AC-2
practice_description: Monitor the use of accounts

capability_description: Account Management | Automated System Account Management
name: AC-2(1)
practice_description: Support the management of system accounts using [Assignment: organization-defined automated mechanisms].

name: AC-2(11)
practice_description: Enforce [Assignment: organization-defined circumstances and/or usage conditions] for [Assignment: organization-defined system accounts].

capability_description: Account Management | Account Monitoring for Atypical Usage
name: AC-2(12)
practice_description: Monitor system accounts for [Assignment: organization-defined atypical usage]; and Report atypical usage of system accounts to [Assignment: organization-defined personnel or roles].

capability_description: Account Management | Disable Accounts for High-risk Individuals
name: AC-2(13)
practice_description: Disable accounts of individuals within [Assignment: organization-defined time period] of discovery of [Assignment: organization-defined significant risks].

capability_description: Account Management | Automated Temporary and Emergency Account Management
name: AC-2(2)
practice_description: Automatically [Assignment: remove, disable] temporary and emergency accounts after [Assignment: organization-defined time period for each type of account].

name: AC-2(3)
practice_description: Disable accounts within an organization-defined time period when the accounts: Have expired; Are no longer associated with a user or individual; Are in violation of organizational policy; or Have been inactive for an organization-defined time period.

name: AC-2(4)
practice_description: Automatically audit account creation, modification, enabling, disabling, and removal actions.

name: AC-2(5)
practice_description: Require that users log out when [Assignment: organization-defined time period of expected inactivity or description of when to log out].

name: AC-3
practice_description: The information system enforces approved authorizations for logical access to information and system resources in accordance with applicable access control policies.

name: AC-4
practice_description: The information system enforces approved authorizations for controlling the flow of information within the system based on organization-defined information flow control policies.

capability_description: Information Flow Enforcement | Flow Control of Encrypted Information
name: AC-4(4)
practice_description: The organization defines procedures or methods to be employed by the information system to prevent encrypted information from bypassing content-checking mechanisms, such as decrypting the information, blocking the flow of the encrypted information, and/or terminating communications sessions attempting to pass encrypted information.

name: AC-5
practice_description: The organization separates organization-defined duties of individuals.

name: AC-6
practice_description: The organization employs the concept of least privilege, allowing only authorized accesses for users (and processes acting on behalf of users) which are necessary to accomplish assigned tasks in accordance with organizational missions and business functions.

capability_description: Least Privilege | Authorize Access to Security Functions
name: AC-6(1)
practice_description: The organization defines the security functions (deployed in hardware, software, and firmware) for which access must be explicitly authorized.

capability_description: Least Privilege | Prohibit Non-privileged Users from Executing Privileged Functions
name: AC-6(10)
practice_description: The information system prevents non-privileged users from executing privileged functions to include disabling, circumventing, or altering implemented security safeguards/countermeasures.

capability_description: Least Privilege | Non-privileged Access for Nonsecurity Functions
name: AC-6(2)
practice_description: The organization requires that users of information system accounts or roles, with access to organization-defined security functions or security-relevant information, use non-privileged accounts, or roles, when accessing nonsecurity functions.

capability_description: Least Privilege | Network Access to Privileged Commands
name: AC-6(3)
practice_description: The organization authorizes network access to organization-defined privileged commands only for organization-defined compelling operational needs.

name: AC-6(5)
practice_description: The organization defines the personnel or roles to whom privileged accounts are to be restricted on the information system.

capability_description: Least Privilege | Review of User Privileges
name: AC-6(7)
practice_description: The organization defines the frequency on which it conducts reviews of the privileges assigned to organization-defined roles or classes of users.

capability_description: Least Privilege | Log Use of Privileged Functions
name: AC-6(9)
practice_description: The information system audits the execution of privileged functions.

name: AC-7
practice_description: The organization defines the maximum number of consecutive invalid logon attempts to the information system by a user during an organization-defined time period.

name: AC-8
practice_description: The information system displays an organization-defined system use notification message or banner before granting access to the system that provides privacy and security notices consistent with applicable federal laws, Executive Orders, directives, policies, regulations, standards, and guidance.

name: AC-10
practice_description: The information system limits the number of concurrent sessions for each organization-defined account and/or account type to an organization-defined number of sessions.

name: AC-11(1)
practice_description: The information system conceals, via the session lock, information previously visible on the display with a publicly viewable image.

name: AC-11
practice_description: The information system provides the capability for users to directly initiate session lock mechanisms.

name: AC-12
practice_description: The organization defines the conditions or trigger events requiring session disconnect to be employed by the information system when automatically terminating a user session.

capability_description: Permitted Actions Without Identification or Authentication
name: AC-14
practice_description: The organization identifies and defines organization-defined user actions that can be performed on the information system without identification or authentication consistent with organizational missions/business functions.

name: AC-17(1)
practice_description: Employ automated mechanisms to monitor and control remote access methods.

capability_description: Remote Access | Protection of Confidentiality and Integrity Using Encryption
name: AC-17(2)
practice_description: Implement cryptographic mechanisms to protect the confidentiality and integrity of remote access sessions.

capability_description: Remote Access | Managed Access Control Points
name: AC-17(3)
practice_description: Route remote accesses through authorized and managed network access control points.

capability_description: Remote Access | Privileged Commands and Access
name: AC-17(4)
practice_description: Authorize the execution of privileged commands and access to security-relevant information via remote access only in a format that provides assessable evidence and for the following needs: [Assignment: organization-defined needs]; and Document the rationale for remote access in the security plan for the system.

name: AC-17
practice_description:
Establish and document usage restrictions, configuration/connection requirements, and implementation guidance for each type of remote access allowed; and
Authorize each type of remote access to the system prior to allowing such connections.

name: AC-18(1)
practice_description: Protect wireless access to the system using authentication of [Assignment (one or more): users, devices] and encryption.

name: AC-18(3)
practice_description: Disable, when not intended for use, wireless networking capabilities embedded within system components prior to issuance and deployment.

capability_description: Wireless Access | Restrict Configurations by Users
name: AC-18(4)
practice_description: Identify and explicitly authorize users allowed to independently configure wireless networking capabilities.

capability_description: Wireless Access | Antennas and Transmission Power Levels
name: AC-18(5)
practice_description: Select radio antennas and calibrate transmission power levels to reduce the probability that signals from wireless access points can be received outside of organization-controlled boundaries.

name: AC-18
practice_description:
Establish configuration requirements, connection requirements, and implementation guidance for each type of wireless access; and
Authorize each type of wireless access to the system prior to allowing such connections.

capability_description: Access Control for Mobile Devices | Full Device or Container-based Encryption
name: AC-19(5)
practice_description: Employ [Assignment: full-device encryption, container-based encryption] to protect the confidentiality and integrity of information on [Assignment: organization-defined mobile devices].

name: AC-19
practice_description:
Establish configuration requirements, connection requirements, and implementation guidance for organization-controlled mobile devices, to include when such devices are outside of controlled areas; and
Authorize the connection of mobile devices to organizational systems.

capability_description: Use of External Systems | Limits on Authorized Use
name: AC-20(1)
practice_description: The organization permits authorized individuals to use an external information system to access the information system only when the organization verifies the implementation of required security controls on the external system as specified in the organization^s information security policy and security plan.

capability_description: Use of External Systems | Portable Storage Devices - Restricted Use
name: AC-20(2)
practice_description: Restrict the use of organization-controlled portable storage devices by authorized individuals on external systems using [Assignment: organization-defined restrictions]

name: AC-20
practice_description: The organization establishes terms and conditions, consistent with any trust relationships established with other organizations owning, operating, and/or maintaining external information systems, allowing authorized individuals to access the information system from the external information systems.

name: AC-21
practice_description: The organization facilitates information sharing by enabling authorized users to determine whether access authorizations assigned to the sharing partner match the access restrictions on the information for organization-defined information circumstances where user discretion is required.

name: AC-22
practice_description: The organization designates individuals authorized to post information onto a publicly accessible information system.

name: CA-1
practice_description: The organization defines the personnel or roles to whom security assessment and authorization policy is to be disseminated.

name: CA-2
practice_description: The organization develops a security assessment plan for the information system and its environment of operation.

name: CA-2(1)
practice_description: The organization employs assessors or assessment teams with an organization-defined level of independence to conduct security control assessments of organizational information systems.

name: CA-2(2)
practice_description: The organization includes, as part of security control assessments announced or unannounced, one or more of the following: in-depth monitoring; vulnerability scanning; malicious user testing; insider threat assessment; performance/load testing; and organization-defined other forms of security assessment on an organization-defined frequency.

name: CA-3
practice_description: The organization authorizes connections from the information system to other information systems through the use of Interconnection Security Agreements.

name: CA-3(6)
practice_description: Verify that individuals or systems transferring data between interconnecting systems have the requisite authorizations (i.e., write permissions or privileges) prior to accepting such data.

name: CA-5
practice_description: The organization develops a plan of action and milestones for the information system to document the organization^s planned remedial actions to correct weaknesses or deficiencies noted during the assessment of the security controls and to reduce or eliminate known vulnerabilities in the system.

name: CA-6
practice_description: The organization assigns a senior-level executive or manager as the authorizing official for the information system.

name: CA-7
practice_description: The organization develops a continuous monitoring strategy.

name: CA-7(1)
practice_description: The organization employs assessors or assessment teams with an organization-defined level of independence to monitor the security controls in the information system on an ongoing basis.

name: CA-7(4)
practice_description: Ensure risk monitoring is an integral part of the continuous monitoring strategy that includes the following: Effectiveness monitoring; Compliance monitoring; and Change monitoring.

name: CA-8
practice_description: The organization conducts penetration testing in accordance with organization-defined frequency on organization-defined information systems or system components.

capability_description: Penetration Testing | Independent Penetration Testing Agent or Team
name: CA-8(1)
practice_description: The organization employs an independent penetration agent or penetration team to perform penetration testing on the information system or system components.

name: CA-9
practice_description: The organization authorizes internal connections of organization-defined information system components or classes of components to the information system.

name: AU-1
practice_description: The organization defines the organizational personnel or roles to whom the audit and accountability policy is to be disseminated.

name: AU-2
practice_description: The organization defines the information system auditable events.

name: AU-3
practice_description: The information system generates audit records containing information that establishes what type of event occurred.

capability_description: Content of Audit Records | Additional Audit Information
name: AU-3(1)
practice_description: The information system generates audit records containing the organization-defined additional, more detailed information that is to be included in the audit records.

name: AU-4
practice_description: The organization allocates audit record storage capacity in accordance with organization-defined audit record storage requirements.

capability_description: Response to Audit Logging Process Failures
name: AU-5
practice_description: The organization defines actions to be taken by the information system upon audit failure (e.g., shut down information system, overwrite oldest audit records, stop generating audit records).

capability_description: Response to Audit Logging Process Failures | Storage Capacity Warning
name: AU-5(1)
practice_description: The organization defines the personnel, roles and/or locations to receive a warning when allocated audit record storage volume reaches a defined percentage of maximum audit records storage capacity.

capability_description: Response to Audit Logging Process Failures | Real-time Alerts
name: AU-5(2)
practice_description: The organization defines the real-time period within which the information system is to provide an alert when organization-defined audit failure events occur.

capability_description: Audit Record Review, Analysis, and Reporting
name: AU-6
practice_description: The organization defines the personnel or roles to receive the reports of organization-defined inappropriate or unusual activity.

capability_description: Audit Record Review, Analysis, and Reporting | Automated Process Integration
name: AU-6(1)
practice_description: The organization employs automated mechanisms to integrate audit review and analysis to support organizational processes for investigation of and response to suspicious activities.

capability_description: Audit Record Review, Analysis, and Reporting | Correlate Audit Record Repositories
name: AU-6(3)
practice_description: The organization analyzes and correlates audit records across different repositories to gain organization-wide situational awareness.

capability_description: Audit Record Review, Analysis, and Reporting | Integrated Analysis of Audit Records
name: AU-6(5)
practice_description: The organization defines the data/information to be collected from other sources to enhance its ability to identify inappropriate or unusual activity.

capability_description: Audit Record Review, Analysis, and Reporting | Correlation with Physical Monitoring
name: AU-6(6)
practice_description: The organization correlates information from audit records with information obtained from monitoring physical access to further enhance the ability to identify suspicious, inappropriate, unusual, or malevolent activity.

capability_description: Audit Record Reduction and Report Generation
name: AU-7
practice_description: The information system provides a report generation capability that does not alter original content or time ordering of audit records.

capability_description: Audit Record Reduction and Report Generation | Automatic Processing
name: AU-7(1)
practice_description: The information system provides the capability to process audit records for events of interest based on organization-defined audit fields within audit records.

name: AU-8
practice_description: The information system records time stamps for audit records that can be mapped to Coordinated Universal Time (UTC) or Greenwich Mean Time (GMT).

name: AU-9
practice_description: The information system protects audit tools from unauthorized deletion.

capability_description: Protection of Audit Information | Store on Separate Physical Systems or Components
name: AU-9(2)
practice_description: The information system backs up audit records on an organization-defined frequency onto a different system or system component than the system or component being audited.

capability_description: Protection of Audit Information | Cryptographic Protection
name: AU-9(3)
practice_description: The information system implements cryptographic mechanisms to protect the integrity of audit information.

capability_description: Protection of Audit Information | Access by Subset of Privileged Users
name: AU-9(4)
practice_description: The organization defines the subset of privileged users who will be authorized access to the management of audit functionality.

name: AU-10
practice_description: The organization defines the actions to be covered by non-repudiation.

name: AU-11
practice_description: The organization defines the time period for retention of audit records, which is consistent with its records retention policy, to provide support for after-the-fact investigations of security incidents and meet regulatory and organizational information retention requirements.

capability_description: Audit Record Generation | System-wide and Time-correlated Audit Trail
name: AU-12(1)
practice_description: The organization defines the level of tolerance for relationship between time stamps of individual records in the audit trail that will be used for correlation.

capability_description: Audit Record Generation | Changes by Authorized Individuals
name: AU-12(3)
practice_description: The organization defines the selectable event criteria to be used as the basis for changes to the auditing to be performed on organization-defined information system components, by organization-defined individuals or roles, within organization-defined time thresholds.

name: AU-12
practice_description: The information system provides audit record generation capability for the auditable events defined in AU-2 a. at organization-defined information system components.

name: AT-1
practice_description: The organization defines the personnel or roles to whom the security awareness and training policy is disseminated.

name: AT-2
practice_description: The organization provides basic security awareness training to information system users (including managers, senior executives, and contractors) as part of initial training for new users.

capability_description: Literacy Training and Awareness | Insider Threat
name: AT-2(2)
practice_description: The organization includes security awareness training on recognizing and reporting potential indicators of insider threat.

capability_description: Literacy Training and Awareness | Social Engineering and Mining
name: AT-2(3)
practice_description: Provide literacy training on recognizing and reporting potential and actual instances of social engineering and social mining.

name: AT-3
practice_description: The organization provides role-based security training to personnel with assigned security roles and responsibilities before authorizing access to the information system or performing assigned duties.

name: AT-4
practice_description: The organization documents individual information system security training activities, including basic security awareness training and specific information system security training.

name: CM-1
practice_description: The organization defines the organizational personnel or roles to whom the configuration management policy is to be disseminated.

name: CM-2
practice_description: The organization maintains, under configuration control, a current baseline configuration of the information system.

capability_description: Baseline Configuration | Automation Support for Accuracy and Currency
name: CM-2(2)
practice_description: The organization employs automated mechanisms to maintain a readily available baseline configuration of the information system.

capability_description: Baseline Configuration | Retention of Previous Configurations
name: CM-2(3)
practice_description: The organization retains organization-defined previous versions of baseline configurations of the information system to support rollback.

capability_description: Baseline Configuration | Configure Systems and Components for High-risk Areas
name: CM-2(7)
practice_description: The organization defines the information systems, system components, or devices that are to have organization-defined configurations applied when located in areas of significant risk.

name: CM-3
practice_description: The organization coordinates and provides oversight for configuration change control activities through an organization-defined configuration change control element (e.g., committee, board) that convenes at the organization-defined frequency and/or for any organization-defined configuration change conditions.

capability_description: Configuration Change Control | Automated Documentation, Notification, and Prohibition of Changes
name: CM-3(1)
practice_description: The organization employs automated mechanisms to notify organization-defined personnel when approved changes to the information system are completed.

capability_description: Configuration Change Control | Testing, Validation, and Documentation of Changes
name: CM-3(2)
practice_description: The organization tests changes to the information system before implementing the changes on the operational system.

capability_description: Configuration Change Control | Security and Privacy Representatives
name: CM-3(4)
practice_description: The organization requires an information security representative to be a member of the organization-defined configuration change control element.

name: CM-3(6)
practice_description: The organization defines the security safeguards that are to be provided by the cryptographic mechanisms which are employed by the organization.

name: CM-4
practice_description: The organization analyzes changes to the information system to determine potential security impacts prior to change implementation.

name: CM-4(1)
practice_description: The organization, when analyzing changes to the information system, looks for security impacts due to flaws, weaknesses, incompatibility, or intentional malice.

name: CM-4(2)
practice_description: The organization, after the information system is changed, checks the security functions to verify the functions are producing the desired outcome with regard to meeting the security requirements for the system.

name: CM-5
practice_description: The organization enforces logical access restrictions associated with changes to the information system.

capability_description: Access Restrictions for Change | Automated Access Enforcement and Audit Records
name: CM-5(1)
practice_description: The information system enforces access restrictions.

name: CM-6
practice_description: The organization defines security configuration checklists to be used to establish and document configuration settings for the information system technology products employed.

capability_description: Configuration Settings | Automated Management, Application, and Verification
name: CM-6(1)
practice_description: The organization employs automated mechanisms to centrally manage configuration settings for organization-defined information system components.

capability_description: Configuration Settings | Respond to Unauthorized Changes
name: CM-6(2)
practice_description: The organization employs organization-defined security safeguards to respond to unauthorized changes to organization-defined configuration settings.

name: CM-7
practice_description: The organization configures the information system to provide only essential capabilities.

name: CM-7(1)
practice_description: The organization reviews the information system per organization-defined frequency to identify unnecessary and nonsecure functions, ports, protocols, and services.

name: CM-7(2)
practice_description: The information system prevents program execution in accordance with organization-defined policies regarding software program usage and restrictions, and/or rules authorizing the terms and conditions of software program usage.

name: CM-7(5)
practice_description: The organization reviews and updates the list of authorized software programs per organization-defined frequency.

name: CM-8
practice_description: The organization reviews and updates the information system component inventory per organization-defined frequency.

capability_description: System Component Inventory | Updates During Installation and Removal
name: CM-8(1)
practice_description: The organization updates the inventory of information system components as an integral part of component installations.

name: CM-8(2)
practice_description: The organization employs automated mechanisms to help maintain a readily available inventory of information system components.

capability_description: System Component Inventory | Automated Unauthorized Component Detection
name: CM-8(3)
practice_description: When unauthorized hardware, software, and firmware components are detected within the information system, the organization takes action to disable network access by such components, isolates the components, and/or notifies organization-defined personnel or roles.

name: CM-8(4)
practice_description: The organization includes, in the information system component inventory information, a means for identifying by name, position, and/or role, individuals responsible/accountable for administering those components.

name: CM-9
practice_description: The organization develops a configuration management plan for the information system that places the configuration items under configuration management.

name: CM-10
practice_description: The organization documents the use of peer-to-peer file sharing technology to ensure that this capability is not used for the unauthorized distribution, display, performance, or reproduction of copyrighted work.

name: CM-11
practice_description: The organization monitors software installation policy compliance per an organization-defined frequency.

name: CP-1
practice_description: The organization develops and documents a contingency planning policy that addresses purpose, scope, roles, responsibilities, management commitment, coordination among organizational entities, and compliance.

name: CP-2
practice_description: The organization develops a contingency plan for the information system that addresses maintaining essential business functions despite an information system disruption.

capability_description: Contingency Plan | Coordinate with Related Plans
name: CP-2(1)
practice_description: The organization coordinates contingency plan development with organizational elements responsible for related plans.

name: CP-2(2)
practice_description: The organization conducts capacity planning so that necessary capacity for environmental support exists during contingency operations.

capability_description: Contingency Plan | Resume Mission and Business Functions
name: CP-2(3)
practice_description: The organization defines the time period for planning the resumption of essential missions as a result of contingency plan activation.

capability_description: Contingency Plan | Continue Mission and Business Functions
name: CP-2(5)
practice_description: The organization sustains operational continuity of essential business functions until full information system restoration at primary processing and/or storage sites.

name: CP-2(8)
practice_description: The organization identifies critical information system assets supporting essential business functions.

name: CP-3
practice_description: The organization provides refresher contingency training to information system users consistent with assigned roles and responsibilities in accordance with organization-defined frequency.

name: CP-3(1)
practice_description: The organization incorporates simulated events into contingency training to facilitate effective response by personnel in crisis situations.

name: CP-4
practice_description: The organization initiates corrective actions, if needed, after reviewing the contingency plan test results.

capability_description: Contingency Plan Testing | Coordinate with Related Plans
name: CP-4(1)
practice_description: The organization coordinates contingency plan testing with organizational elements responsible for related plans.

capability_description: Contingency Plan Testing | Alternate Processing Site
name: CP-4(2)
practice_description: The organization tests the contingency plan at the alternate processing site to familiarize contingency personnel with the facility and available resources.

name: CP-6
practice_description: The organization ensures that the alternate storage site provides information security safeguards equivalent to that of the primary site.

capability_description: Alternate Storage Site | Separation from Primary Site
name: CP-6(1)
practice_description: The organization identifies an alternate storage site that is separated from the primary storage site to reduce susceptibility to the same threats.

capability_description: Alternate Storage Site | Recovery Time and Recovery Point Objectives
name: CP-6(2)
practice_description: The organization configures the alternate storage site to facilitate recovery operations in accordance with recovery time and recovery point objectives.

name: CP-6(3)
practice_description: The organization outlines explicit mitigation actions for organization identified accessibility problems to the alternate storage site in the event of an area-wide disruption or disaster.

name: CP-7
practice_description: The organization ensures that the alternate processing site provides information security safeguards equivalent to that of the primary site.

capability_description: Alternate Processing Site | Separation from Primary Site
name: CP-7(1)
practice_description: The organization identifies an alternate processing site that is separated from the primary processing site to reduce susceptibility to the same threats.

name: CP-7(2)
practice_description: The organization outlines explicit mitigation actions for organization-identified potential accessibility problems to the alternate processing site in the event of an area-wide disruption or disaster.

capability_description: Alternate Processing Site | Priority of Service
name: CP-7(3)
practice_description: The organization develops alternate processing site agreements that contain priority-of-service provisions in accordance with the organizational availability requirements (including recovery time objectives).

capability_description: Alternate Processing Site | Preparation for Use
name: CP-7(4)
practice_description: The organization prepares the alternate processing site so that it is ready to be used as the operational site supporting essential missions.

name: CP-8
practice_description: The organization defines the information system operations to be resumed for essential business functions within the organization-defined time period when the primary telecommunications capabilities are unavailable at either the primary or alternate processing or storage sites.

capability_description: Telecommunications Services | Priority of Service Provisions
name: CP-8(1)
practice_description: The organization develops primary telecommunications service agreements that contain priority-of-service provisions in accordance with the organization^s availability requirements (including recovery time objectives).

capability_description: Telecommunications Services | Single Points of Failure
name: CP-8(2)
practice_description: The organization obtains alternate telecommunications services to reduce the likelihood of sharing a single point of failure with primary telecommunications services.

capability_description: Telecommunications Services | Separation of Primary and Alternate Providers
name: CP-8(3)
practice_description: The organization obtains alternate telecommunications services from providers that are separated from primary service providers to reduce susceptibility to the same threats.

name: CP-8(4)
practice_description: The organization requires primary telecommunications service providers to have contingency plans.

name: CP-9
practice_description: The organization protects the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of backup information at storage locations.

capability_description: System Backup | Testing for Reliability and Integrity
name: CP-9(1)
practice_description: The organization defines the frequency with which to test backup information to verify media reliability and information integrity.

capability_description: System Backup | Test Restoration Using Sampling
name: CP-9(2)
practice_description: The organization uses a sample of backup information in the restoration of selected information system functions as part of contingency plan testing.

capability_description: System Backup | Separate Storage for Critical Information
name: CP-9(3)
practice_description: The organization stores backup copies of organization-defined critical information system software and other security-related information in a separate facility or in a fire-rated container that is not collocated with the operational system.

capability_description: System Backup | Transfer to Alternate Storage Site
name: CP-9(5)
practice_description: The organization defines the time period and transfer rate of the information system backup information to the alternate storage site consistent with the recovery time and recovery point objectives.

name: CP-9(8)
practice_description: The organization enforces dual authorization for the deletion or destruction of organization-defined backup information.

capability_description: System Recovery and Reconstitution | Transaction Recovery
name: CP-10(2)
practice_description: The information system implements transaction recovery for systems that are transaction-based.

capability_description: System Recovery and Reconstitution | Restore Within Time Period
name: CP-10(4)
practice_description: The organization defines restoration time periods within which to restore information system components from configuration-controlled and integrity-protected information representing a known, operational state for the components.

name: CP-10
practice_description: The organization provides for the recovery and reconstitution of the information system to a known state after a failure.

name: IA-1
practice_description: The organization defines the personnel or roles to be recipients of the identification and authentication policy and the procedures to facilitate the implementation of the identification and authentication policy and associated identification and authentication controls.

name: IA-2
practice_description: The information system uniquely identifies and authenticates organizational users (or processes acting on behalf of organizational users).

capability_description: Identification and Authentication (Organizational Users) | Multi-factor Authentication to Privileged Accounts
name: IA-2(1)
practice_description: The information system implements multifactor authentication for network access to privileged accounts.

capability_description: Identification and Authentication (Organizational Users) | Acceptance of PIV Credentials
name: IA-2(12)
practice_description: The information system electronically verifies Personal Identity Verification (PIV) credentials.

capability_description: Identification and Authentication (Organizational Users) | Multi-factor Authentication to Non-privileged Accounts
name: IA-2(2)
practice_description: The information system implements multifactor authentication for network access to non-privileged accounts.

capability_description: Identification and Authentication (Organizational Users) | Individual Authentication with Group Authentication
name: IA-2(5)
practice_description: The organization requires individuals to be authenticated with an individual authenticator when a group authenticator is employed.

capability_description: Identification and Authentication (Organizational Users) | Access to Accounts ? Replay Resistant
name: IA-2(8)
practice_description: The information system implements replay-resistant authentication mechanisms for network access to privileged accounts.

name: IA-3
practice_description: The information system authenticates an organization-defined list of specific and/or types of devices before establishing a local, remote, or network connection.

name: IA-4
practice_description: The organization manages information system identifiers by disabling the identifier after an organization-defined time period of inactivity.

name: IA-4(4)
practice_description: The organization manages individual identifiers by uniquely identifying each individual by organization-defined characteristics identifying individual status.

name: IA-5
practice_description: The organization manages information system authenticators by implementing administrative procedures for lost/compromised authenticators.

name: IA-5(1)
practice_description: The information system enforces password complexity by the minimum number of upper case characters used.

name: IA-5(2)
practice_description: The information system, for PKI-based authentication, enforces authorized access to the corresponding private key.

name: IA-5(6)
practice_description: The organization protects authenticators commensurate with the security category of the information to which use of the authenticator permits access.

name: IA-6
practice_description: The information system obscures feedback of authentication information during the authentication process to protect the information from possible exploitation/use by unauthorized individuals.

name: IA-7
practice_description: The information system implements mechanisms for authentication to a cryptographic module that meet the requirements of applicable federal laws, Executive Orders, directives, policies, regulations, standards, and guidance for such authentication.

name: IA-8
practice_description: The information system uniquely identifies and authenticates non-organizational users (or processes acting on behalf of non-organizational users).

capability_description: Identification and Authentication (Non-organizational Users) | Acceptance of PIV Credentials from Other Agencies
name: IA-8(1)
practice_description: The information system accepts Personal Identity Verification (PIV) credentials from other federal agencies.

capability_description: Identification and Authentication (Non-organizational Users) | Acceptance of External Authenticators
name: IA-8(2)
practice_description: The information system accepts FICAM-approved third-party credentials.

capability_description: Identification and Authentication (Non-organizational Users) | Use of Defined Profiles
name: IA-8(4)
practice_description: The information system conforms to FICAM-issued profiles.

name: IA-11
practice_description: The organization defines the circumstances or situations under which users will be required to reauthenticate.

name: IR-1
practice_description: The organization defines the personnel or roles to whom the incident response policy is disseminated.

name: IR-2
practice_description: The organization provides incident response training to information system users consistent with assigned roles and responsibilities when required by information system changes.

name: IR-2(1)
practice_description: The organization incorporates simulated events into incident response training to facilitate effective response by personnel in crisis situations.

capability_description: Incident Response Training | Automated Training Environments
name: IR-2(2)
practice_description: The organization employs automated mechanisms to provide a more thorough and realistic incident response training environment.

name: IR-3
practice_description: The organization documents the results of incident response tests.

capability_description: Incident Response Testing | Coordination with Related Plans
name: IR-3(2)
practice_description: The organization coordinates incident response testing with organizational elements responsible for related plans.

name: IR-4
practice_description: The organization implements the resulting incident handling activity changes to incident response procedures, training, and testing/exercises accordingly.

capability_description: Incident Handling | Automated Incident Handling Processes
name: IR-4(1)
practice_description: The organization employs automated mechanisms to support the incident handling process.

name: IR-4(4)
practice_description: The organization correlates incident information and individual incident responses to achieve an organization-wide perspective on incident awareness and response.

capability_description: Incident Handling | Integrated Incident Response Team
name: IR-4(11)
practice_description: Establish and maintain an integrated incident response team that can be deployed to any location identified by the organization in [Assignment: organization-defined time period].

name: IR-5
practice_description: The organization tracks and documents information system security incidents.

capability_description: Incident Monitoring | Automated Tracking, Data Collection, and Analysis
name: IR-5(1)
practice_description: The organization employs automated mechanisms to assist in the tracking of security incidents.

name: IR-6
practice_description: The organization defines authorities to whom security incident information is reported.

name: IR-6(1)
practice_description: The organization employs automated mechanisms to assist in the reporting of security incidents.

name: IR-6(3)
practice_description: The organization provides security incident information to other organizations involved in the supply chain for information systems or information system components related to the incident.

name: IR-7
practice_description: The organization provides an incident response support resource, integral to the organizational incident response capability, that offers advice and assistance to users of the information system for the handling and reporting of security incidents.

capability_description: Incident Response Assistance | Automation Support for Availability of Information and Support
name: IR-7(1)
practice_description: The organization employs automated mechanisms to increase the availability of incident response-related information and support.

name: IR-8
practice_description: The organization defines personnel or roles to review and approve the incident response plan.

name: MA-1
practice_description: The organization defines the personnel or roles to whom a system maintenance policy is disseminated.

name: MA-2
practice_description: The organization approves and monitors all maintenance activities, whether performed on site or remotely and whether the equipment is serviced on site or removed to another location.

name: MA-2(2)
practice_description: The organization employs automated mechanisms to schedule, conduct, and document repairs.

name: MA-3
practice_description: The organization monitors information system maintenance tools.

name: MA-3(1)
practice_description: The organization inspects the maintenance tools carried into a facility by maintenance personnel for improper or unauthorized modifications.

name: MA-3(2)
practice_description: The organization checks media containing diagnostic and test programs for malicious code before the media are used in the information system.

name: MA-3(3)
practice_description: The organization defines the personnel or roles who can provide an exemption that explicitly authorizes removal of equipment from the facility.

name: MA-3(4)
practice_description: The information system restricts the use of maintenance tools to authorized personnel only.

name: MA-4
practice_description: The organization terminates sessions and network connections when nonlocal maintenance is completed.

capability_description: Nonlocal Maintenance | Comparable Security and Sanitization
name: MA-4(3)
practice_description: The organization, before removal from organizational facilities, and after the service is performed, inspects and sanitizes the component (with regard to potentially malicious software) before reconnecting the component to the information system.

name: MA-5
practice_description: The organization designates organizational personnel with required access authorizations and technical competence to supervise the maintenance activities of personnel who do not possess the required access authorizations.

capability_description: Maintenance Personnel | Individuals Without Appropriate Access
name: MA-5(1)
practice_description: The organization implements procedures for the use of maintenance personnel that lack appropriate security clearances or are not U.S. citizens.

name: MA-6
practice_description: The organization defines a time period for obtaining maintenance support and/or spare parts for organization-defined information system components after a failure.

name: MP-1
practice_description: The organization develops and documents a media protection policy that addresses purpose, scope, roles, responsibilities, management commitment, coordination among organizational entities, and compliance.

name: MP-2
practice_description: The organization restricts access to organization-defined types of digital and/or non-digital media to organization-defined personnel or roles.

name: MP-3
practice_description: The organization marks information system media indicating the distribution limitations, handling caveats, and applicable security markings (if any) of the information.

name: MP-4
practice_description: The organization protects information system media until the media are destroyed or sanitized using approved equipment, techniques, and procedures.

name: MP-5
practice_description: The organization restricts the activities associated with the transport of information system media to authorized personnel.

name: MP-6
practice_description: The organization employs sanitization mechanisms with the strength and integrity commensurate with the security category or classification of the information.

capability_description: Media Sanitization | Review, Approve, Track, Document, and Verify
name: MP-6(1)
practice_description: The organization reviews and approves media sanitization.

name: MP-6(2)
practice_description: The organization defines a frequency for testing sanitization equipment and procedures to verify that the intended sanitization is being achieved.

name: MP-6(3)
practice_description: The organization applies nondestructive sanitization techniques to portable storage devices prior to connecting such devices to the information system in accordance with organization-defined circumstances requiring sanitization of portable storage devices.

name: MP-7
practice_description: The organization restricts or prohibits the use of organization-defined types of information system media on organization-defined information systems or system components using organization-defined security safeguards.

name: PS-1
practice_description: The organization defines the personnel or roles to whom a personnel security policy is disseminated.

name: PS-2
practice_description: The organization assigns a risk designation to all organizational positions.

name: PS-3
practice_description: The organization defines the frequency for rescreening individuals with authorized access to the information system when organization-defined conditions requiring rescreening are met.

name: PS-4
practice_description: The organization, upon termination of individual employment, disables information system access within an organization-defined time period.

name: PS-4(2)
practice_description: The organization employs automated mechanisms to notify organization-defined personnel or roles upon termination of an individual.

name: PS-5
practice_description: The organization reviews and confirms the ongoing operational need for current logical and physical access authorizations to information systems/facilities when individuals are reassigned or transferred to other positions within the organization.

name: PS-6
practice_description: The organization develops and documents access agreements for organizational information systems.

name: PS-7
practice_description: The organization establishes personnel security requirements including security roles and responsibilities for third-party providers.

name: PS-8
practice_description: The organization employs a formal sanctions process for individuals failing to comply with established information security policies and procedures.

name: PS-9
practice_description: Incorporate security and privacy roles and responsibilities into organizational position descriptions.

name: PE-1
practice_description: The organization defines the personnel or roles to whom a physical and environmental protection policy is disseminated.

name: PE-2
practice_description: The organization develops a list of individuals with authorized access to the facility where the information system resides.

name: PE-3
practice_description: The organization enforces physical access authorizations at organization-defined entry/exit points to the facility where the information system resides.

name: PE-3(1)
practice_description: The organization enforces physical access authorizations to the information system in addition to the physical access controls for the facility where the information system resides at organization-defined physical spaces containing one or more components of the information system.

name: PE-4
practice_description: The organization controls physical access to organization-defined information system distribution and transmission lines within organizational facilities using organization-defined security safeguards.

name: PE-5
practice_description: The organization controls physical access to information system output devices to prevent unauthorized individuals from obtaining the output.

name: PE-6
practice_description: The organization coordinates results of reviews and investigations with the organization^s incident response capability.

capability_description: Monitoring Physical Access | Intrusion Alarms and Surveillance Equipment
name: PE-6(1)
practice_description: The organization monitors physical intrusion alarms and surveillance equipment.

capability_description: Monitoring Physical Access | Monitoring Physical Access to Systems
name: PE-6(4)
practice_description: The organization monitors physical access to the information system in addition to the physical access monitoring of the facility as organization-defined physical spaces containing one or more components of the information system.

name: PE-8
practice_description: The organization defines the frequency with which to review the visitor access records for the facility where the information system resides.

capability_description: Visitor Access Records | Automated Records Maintenance and Review
name: PE-8(1)
practice_description: The organization employs automated mechanisms to facilitate the maintenance and review of access records.

name: PE-9
practice_description: The organization protects power equipment and power cabling for the information system from damage and destruction.

name: PE-10
practice_description: The organization provides the capability of shutting off power to the information system or individual system components in emergency situations.

capability_description: Emergency Power | Alternate Power Supply ? Minimal Operational Capability
name: PE-11(1)
practice_description: The organization provides a long-term alternate power supply for the information system that is capable of maintaining minimally required operational capability in the event of an extended loss of the primary power source.

name: PE-11
practice_description: The organization provides a short-term uninterruptible power supply to facilitate an orderly shutdown of the information system and/or transition of the information system to long-term alternate power in the event of a primary power source loss.

name: PE-12
practice_description: The organization employs and maintains automatic emergency lighting for the information system that activates in the event of a power outage or disruption and that covers emergency exits and evacuation routes within the facility.

capability_description: Fire Protection | Detection Systems ? Automatic Activation and Notification
name: PE-13(1)
practice_description: The organization employs fire detection devices/systems for the information system that activate automatically.

capability_description: Fire Protection | Suppression Systems ? Automatic Activation and Notification
name: PE-13(2)
practice_description: The organization employs fire suppression devices/systems for the information system that provide automatic notification of any activation to organization-defined personnel or roles and organization-defined emergency responders.

name: PE-13
practice_description: The organization employs and maintains fire suppression and detection devices/systems for the information system that are supported by an independent energy source.

name: PE-14
practice_description: The organization maintains temperature and humidity levels within the facility where the information system resides at organization-defined acceptable levels.

name: PE-15(1)
practice_description: The organization employs automated mechanisms to detect the presence of water in the vicinity of the information system and alerts organization-defined personnel or roles.

name: PE-15
practice_description: The organization protects the information system from damage resulting from water leakage by providing master shutoff or isolation valves that are accessible.

name: PE-16
practice_description: The organization authorizes organization-defined types of information system components entering and exiting the facility.

name: PE-17
practice_description: The organization employs organization-defined security controls at alternate work sites.

name: PE-18
practice_description: The organization positions information system components within the facility to minimize potential damage from organization-defined physical and environmental hazards.

name: PL-1
practice_description: The organization defines the personnel or roles to whom a security planning policy is disseminated.

name: PL-2
practice_description: The organization develops a security plan for the information system.

name: PL-4
practice_description: The organization establishes the rules describing the responsibilities and expected behavior, with regard to information and information system usage, for individuals requiring access to the information system.

capability_description: Rules of Behavior | Social Media and External Site/Application Usage Restrictions
name: PL-4(1)
practice_description: The organization includes in the rules of behavior explicit restrictions on the use of social media/networking sites.

name: PL-8
practice_description: The organization develops an information security architecture for the information system.

name: RA-1
practice_description: The organization defines the personnel or roles to whom the risk assessment policy is disseminated.

name: RA-2
practice_description: The organization categorizes information and the information system in accordance with applicable federal laws, Executive Orders, directives, policies, regulations, standards, and guidance.

name: RA-3
practice_description: Assess supply chain risks associated with [Assignment: organization-defined systems, system components, and system services]; and Update the supply chain risk assessment [Assignment: organization-defined frequency], when there are significant changes to the relevant supply chain, or when changes to the system, environments of operation, or other conditions may necessitate a change in the supply chain.

capability_description: Risk Assessment | Supply Chain Risk Assessment
name: RA-3(1)
practice_description: The organization conducts an assessment of risk of the information system and the information it processes, stores, or transmits that includes the likelihood and magnitude of harm from the unauthorized access, use, disclosure, disruption, modification, or destruction.

name: RA-5
practice_description: The organization scans for vulnerabilities in the information system and hosted applications on an organization-defined frequency.

capability_description: Vulnerability Monitoring and Scanning | Update Vulnerabilities to Be Scanned
name: RA-5(2)
practice_description: The organization updates the information system vulnerabilities scanned on an organization-defined frequency, prior to a new scan, and/or when new vulnerabilities are identified and reported.

capability_description: Vulnerability Monitoring and Scanning | Discoverable Information
name: RA-5(4)
practice_description: The organization determines what information about the information system is discoverable by adversaries.

capability_description: Vulnerability Monitoring and Scanning | Privileged Access
name: RA-5(5)
practice_description: The information system implements privileged access authorization to organization-identified information system components for selected organization-defined vulnerability scanning activities.

capability_description: Vulnerability Monitoring and Scanning | Public Disclosure Program
name: RA-5(11)
practice_description: Establish a public reporting channel for receiving reports of vulnerabilities in organizational systems and system components.

name: RA-7
practice_description: Respond to findings from security and privacy assessments, monitoring, and audits in accordance with organizational risk tolerance.

name: RA-9
practice_description: Identify critical system components and functions by performing a criticality analysis for [Assignment: organization-defined systems, system components, or system services] at [Assignment: organization-defined decision points in the system development life cycle].

name: SA-1
practice_description: The organization defines the personnel or roles to whom the system and services acquisition policy is disseminated.

name: SA-2
practice_description: The organization determines information security requirements for the information system or information system service in mission/business process planning.

name: SA-3
practice_description: The organization manages the information system using an organization-defined system development life cycle that incorporates information security considerations.

name: SA-4
practice_description: The organization includes the security functional requirements, explicitly or by reference, in the acquisition contract for the information system, system component, or information system service in accordance with applicable federal laws, Executive Orders, directives, policies, regulations, standards, guidelines, and organizational mission/business needs.

capability_description: Acquisition Process | Functional Properties of Controls
name: SA-4(1)
practice_description: The organization requires the developer of the information system, system component, or information system service to provide a description of the functional properties of the security controls to be employed.

capability_description: Acquisition Process | Use of Approved PIV Products
name: SA-4(10)
practice_description: The organization employs only information technology products on the FIPS 201-approved products list for Personal Identity Verification (PIV) capability implemented within organizational information systems.

capability_description: Acquisition Process | Design and Implementation Information for Controls
name: SA-4(2)
practice_description: The organization requires the developer of the information system, system component, or information system service to provide design information for the security controls to be employed that includes security-relevant external system interfaces, high-level design, low-level design, source code, hardware schematics, and/or organization-defined design information at an organization-defined level of detail.

capability_description: Acquisition Process | System, Component, and Service Configurations
name: SA-4(5)
practice_description: The organization requires the developer of the information system, system component, or information system service to deliver the system, component, or service with organization-defined security configurations implemented.

capability_description: Acquisition Process | Functions, Ports, Protocols, and Services in Use
name: SA-4(9)
practice_description: The organization requires the developer of the information system, system component, or information system service to identify early in the system development life cycle, the functions, ports, protocols, and services intended for organizational use.

name: SA-5
practice_description: The organization obtains administrator documentation for the information system, system component, or information system service that describes secure configuration of the system, component, or service.

name: SA-8
practice_description: The organization applies information system security engineering principles in the specification of the information system.

name: SA-9
practice_description: The organization requires that providers of external information system services comply with organizational information security requirements.

capability_description: External System Services | Identification of Functions, Ports, Protocols, and Services
name: SA-9(2)
practice_description: The organization requires providers of organization-defined external information system services to identify the functions, ports, protocols, and other services required for the use of such services.

capability_description: Developer Configuration Management | Software and Firmware Integrity Verification
name: SA-10(1)
practice_description: The organization requires the developer of the information system, system component, or information system service to enable integrity verification of software and firmware components.

name: SA-10
practice_description: The organization requires the developer of the information system, system component, or information system service to perform configuration management during system, component, or service design, development, implementation and/or operation.

name: SA-11
practice_description: The organization requires the developer of the information system, system component, or information system service to create a security assessment plan.

capability_description: Development Process, Standards, and Tools | Criticality Analysis
name: SA-15(3)
practice_description: The organization defines the breadth/depth at which the developer of the information system, system component, or information system service is required to perform a criticality analysis.

name: SA-15
practice_description: The organization requires the developer of the information system, system component, or information system service to follow a documented development process.

name: SA-16
practice_description: The organization requires the developer of the information system, system component, or information system service to provide organization-defined training on the correct use and operation of the implemented security functions, controls, and/or mechanisms.

capability_description: Developer Security and Privacy Architecture and Design
name: SA-17
practice_description: The organization requires the developer of the information system, system component, or information system service to produce a design specification and security architecture.

name: SA-21
practice_description: The organization defines the information system, system component, or information system service which requires the information system developer to have appropriate access authorizations and satisfy additional personnel screening criteria.

name: SA-22
practice_description: The organization replaces information system components when support for the components is no longer available from the developer, vendor, or manufacturer.

name: SC-1
practice_description: The organization defines the personnel or roles to be recipients of the system and communications protection policy.

capability_description: Separation of System and User Functionality
name: SC-2
practice_description: The information system separates user functionality (including user interface services) from information system management functionality.

name: SC-3
practice_description: The information system isolates security functions from nonsecurity functions.

name: SC-4
practice_description: The information system prevents unauthorized and unintended information transfer via shared system resources.

name: SC-5
practice_description: The organization defines the types of denial of service attacks (or provides references to sources of current denial of service attacks) that can be addressed by the information system.

name: SC-7
practice_description: The information system monitors and controls communications at the external boundary of the information system and at key internal boundaries within the system.

name: SC-7(18)
practice_description: The information system fails securely in the event of an operational failure of a boundary protection device.

capability_description: Boundary Protection | Isolation of System Components
name: SC-7(21)
practice_description: The organization defines the information system components supporting organization-defined missions and/or business functions that are to be separated using boundary protection mechanisms.

name: SC-7(3)
practice_description: The organization limits the number of external network connections to the information system.

name: SC-7(4)
practice_description: The organization establishes a traffic flow policy for each managed interface for each external telecommunication service.

capability_description: Boundary Protection | Deny by Default ? Allow by Exception
name: SC-7(5)
practice_description: The information system at managed interfaces denies network communications traffic by default and allows network communications traffic by exception (i.e., deny all, permit by exception).

capability_description: Boundary Protection | Split Tunneling for Remote Devices
name: SC-7(7)
practice_description: The information system, in conjunction with a remote device, prevents the device from simultaneously establishing non-remote connections with the system and communicating via some other connection to resources in external networks.

capability_description: Boundary Protection | Route Traffic to Authenticated Proxy Servers
name: SC-7(8)
practice_description: The information system routes organization-defined internal communications traffic to organization-defined external networks through authenticated proxy servers at managed interfaces.

name: SC-8
practice_description: The information system protects the confidentiality and/or integrity of transmitted information.

capability_description: Transmission Confidentiality and Integrity | Cryptographic Protection
name: SC-8(1)
practice_description: The organization defines the alternative physical safeguards to be employed when cryptographic mechanisms are not implemented to protect information during transmission.

name: SC-10
practice_description: The information system terminates the network connection associated with a communications session at the end of the session or after an organization-defined time period of inactivity.

capability_description: Cryptographic Key Establishment and Management | Availability
name: SC-12(1)
practice_description: The organization maintains availability of information in the event of the loss of cryptographic keys by users.

name: SC-12
practice_description: The organization defines the requirements for cryptographic key generation to be employed within the information system.

name: SC-13
practice_description: The organization defines the cryptographic uses, and type of cryptography required for each use, to be implemented by the information system.

name: SC-15
practice_description: The information system prohibits remote activation of collaborative computing devices, excluding the organization-defined exceptions where remote activation is to be allowed.

name: SC-17
practice_description: The organization issues public key certificates under an organization-defined certificate policy or obtains public key certificates from an approved service provider.

name: SC-18
practice_description: The organization defines acceptable and unacceptable mobile code and mobile code technologies.

name: SC-20
practice_description: The information system provides additional data origin authentication artifacts along with the authoritative name resolution data the system returns in response to external name/address resolution queries.

capability_description: Secure Name/Address Resolution Service (Recursive or Caching Resolver)
name: SC-21
practice_description: The information system requests data origin authentication verification on the name/address resolution responses the system receives from authoritative sources.

capability_description: Architecture and Provisioning for Name/Address Resolution Service
name: SC-22
practice_description: The information systems that collectively provide name/address resolution service for an organization are fault-tolerant.

name: SC-23
practice_description: The information system protects the authenticity of communications sessions.

name: SC-24
practice_description: The information system fails to an organization-defined known-state for organization-defined types of failures.

capability_description: Protection of Information at Rest | Cryptographic Protection
name: SC-28(1)
practice_description: The organization defines the information at rest for which cryptographic mechanisms will be implemented.

name: SC-28
practice_description: The information system protects the confidentiality and/or integrity of organization-defined information at rest.

name: SC-39
practice_description: The information system maintains a separate execution domain for each executing process.

name: SI-1
practice_description: The organization defines the personnel or roles to whom the system and information integrity policy and procedures are to be disseminated.

name: SI-2
practice_description: The organization identifies information system flaws.

capability_description: Flaw Remediation | Automated Flaw Remediation Status
name: SI-2(2)
practice_description: The organization employs automated mechanisms on an organization-defined frequency to determine the state of information system components with regard to flaw remediation.

name: SI-3
practice_description: The organization employs malicious code protection mechanisms at information system entry points to detect malicious code.

name: SI-4
practice_description: The organization defines the objectives of monitoring for attacks and indicators of potential attacks on the information system.

capability_description: System Monitoring | Visibility of Encrypted Communications
name: SI-4(10)
practice_description: The organization defines the encrypted communications traffic that is to be visible to organization-defined information system monitoring tools.

name: SI-4(12)
practice_description: The organization employs automated mechanisms to alert security personnel of organization-defined inappropriate or unusual activities with security implications.

name: SI-4(14)
practice_description: The organization employs a wireless intrusion detection system to identify rogue wireless devices and to detect attack attempts and potential compromises/breaches to the information system.

capability_description: System Monitoring | Automated Tools and Mechanisms for Real-time Analysis
name: SI-4(2)
practice_description: The organization employs automated tools to support near real-time analysis of events.

name: SI-4(20)
practice_description: The organization defines additional monitoring to be implemented for privileged users.

name: SI-4(22)
practice_description: The organization defines the authorization or approval process for network services.

capability_description: System Monitoring | Inbound and Outbound Communications Traffic
name: SI-4(4)
practice_description: The organization defines the frequency on which it will monitor inbound communications for unusual or unauthorized activities or conditions.

name: SI-4(5)
practice_description: The organization defines indicators of compromise or potential compromise to the security of the information system which will result in information system alerts being provided to organization-defined personnel or roles.

name: SI-5
practice_description: The organization receives information system security alerts, advisories, and directives from organization-defined external organizations on an ongoing basis.

capability_description: Security Alerts, Advisories, and Directives | Automated Alerts and Advisories
name: SI-5(1)
practice_description: The organization employs automated mechanisms to make security alert and advisory information available throughout the organization.

name: SI-6
practice_description: The organization defines the security functions that require verification of correct operation.

name: SI-7
practice_description: The organization defines the software, firmware, and information which will be subjected to integrity verification tools to detect unauthorized changes.

capability_description: Software, Firmware, and Information Integrity | Integrity Checks
name: SI-7(1)
practice_description: The organization defines the software on which integrity checks will be performed.

capability_description: Software, Firmware, and Information Integrity | Code Authentication
name: SI-7(15)
practice_description: The organization defines the software or firmware components on which cryptographic mechanisms are to be implemented to support authentication prior to installation.

capability_description: Software, Firmware, and Information Integrity | Automated Notifications of Integrity Violations
name: SI-7(2)
practice_description: The organization employs automated tools that provide notification to organization-defined personnel or roles upon discovering discrepancies during integrity verification.

capability_description: Software, Firmware, and Information Integrity | Automated Response to Integrity Violations
name: SI-7(5)
practice_description: The organization defines the security safeguards that are to be employed when integrity violations are discovered.

capability_description: Software, Firmware, and Information Integrity | Integration of Detection and Response
name: SI-7(7)
practice_description: The organization defines the unauthorized security-relevant changes to the information system that are to be incorporated into the organizational incident response capability.

name: SI-8
practice_description: The organization employs spam protection mechanisms at information system entry points to detect and take action on unsolicited messages.

name: SI-8(2)
practice_description: The information system automatically updates spam protection mechanisms.

name: SI-10
practice_description: The information system checks the validity of organization-defined inputs.

name: SI-11
practice_description: The information system generates error messages that provide information necessary for corrective actions without revealing information that could be exploited by adversaries.

name: SI-12
practice_description: The organization handles information within the information system and information output from the system in accordance with applicable federal laws, Executive Orders, directives, policies, regulations, standards, and operational requirements.

name: SI-16
practice_description: The organization defines the security safeguards to be implemented to protect the information system^s memory from unauthorized code execution.
